invariants: All mutation goes through applyCommand (src/v3/reducer.ts) — no direct state mutation elsewhere

2026-07-18 — Determinism invariant SCOPED (V3.1 sprint; docs/ROADMAP-V3.1-UI.md §3): the 'no Math.random/Date.now under src/' rule is ENGINE+SIM scoped (src/v2, src/v3, src/utils). The presentation layer (src/ui-v3) MAY use requestAnimationFrame / GSAP internal timing, but still NO Math.random — presentational jitter uses a dedicated SeededRandom instance that never touches GameState — and no timing value ever feeds back into game state or commands.

Determinism §7 D1–D9: tokens are pre-bound & fogged via a namespaced SeededRandom(hash(seed, nodeId)) (D1/D9); observableState(state, viewerSeat) redacts token content AND the seed (D2) — it never leaks unflipped tokens or the seed; AI/human/wraith all decide from observableState (a pure f(observableState, seed))

gotchas: vitest output buffers when piped through `tail` — redirect to a file, or use the JSON reporter (verify.mjs does)
Run `npm run verify` to (re)populate the lastVerified block; never hand-edit lastVerified/dirty (script-owned by verify.mjs)

verify.mjs now lints `eslint src/ tests/` (whole repo) and runs `vitest run tests` (the FULL suite — utils/v2/v3, all green, ~1,14x tests). The v3 UI lives in src/ui-v3 (entry index-v3.html) and IS in scope; tsc --noEmit covers all of src/. Validate UI changes with `npm run dev` / `npx vite build`.

ACTION pointer quirk (inherited from v2): after the LAST player in turnOrder exhausts actions, advanceActivePlayer leaves activePlayerIndex ON that player (turnOrderPosition === turnOrder.length). Any per-player action loop must ALSO stop on actionsRemaining===0, not just the index — else it re-enters a 0-action player (runAITurn handles this).

BUNDLE BUDGET: `npm run budget` (scripts/check-budget.mjs, T-206) guards the ≤3 MB M2 exit metric. It runs a CLEAN `vite build` itself (emptyOutDir wipes stale `tsc` output) and measures the resulting dist/ (~444 KB, ~2.6 MB headroom). NEVER measure dist/ after `npm run build`/`tsc` — that leaves ~3 MB of compiled-JS noise and would falsely fail.

BYTE-IDENTICAL SCOPE: the competitive (flag-off) sim is byte-identical to sim-results/sample-v3; the difficulty `warlord` tier = the DEFAULT = the locked balance (byte-identical, re-verified). The two sim-neutral human-only commands SET_WRAITH_INPUT / SET_BEQUEST are UI-only and do NOT perturb the sim. Herald default-OFF (3-archetype); enabling Herald+PARLEY is the advanced path.
openRisks: RE-BROKEN THEN RESOLVED 2026-07-22 (T-301 fix round 1) — SHOTS:V3 WRAITH SCENE AFTER THE T-239 RE-LOCK: `npm run shots:v3` was again exiting 1 ('MISSING 1 screen(s): wraith'). The earlier T-225 fix (below) and the T-237 re-cover (seed 13) were both invalidated by later board/tunable work and the re-break was never recorded. ROOT CAUSE (measured, not guessed): a mid-game Warlord elimination — the Wraith scene's precondition — is close to non-existent at the shots configuration (4p / competitive / warlord) under the T-239 re-lock's cheaper doom path (DOOM_COST_MARCH 14->11, SPREAD_AMOUNT_BASE 2.6->2.2): the dark completes doom before anyone is deposed. Evidence: the T-239 sweep itself records 0.07 eliminations/game pooled (~0.04 in the 4p cell), elimination Act mix 0/3/288 (Whisper/March/Reckoning) and mean earliest deposal round 12.8 of a 14-round cap (i.e. usually simultaneous with the ending, which the shots signature excludes because the Wraiths block must render PRE-endgame); a fresh engine probe (playHeadlessGame, 60 seeds/cell) found 0/60 games with any wraith at 4p-warlord vs 10/60 at 3p; and a full browser sweep of the real shots driver over seeds 1-200 found ZERO covering seeds in 1-40 (the old list's whole range) and 10 covering seeds above it. FIX: SEED_LIST in scripts/shots-v3.mjs is now [42, 11, 44, 96, 124] — 42/11 still yield the other six screens, 44 yields the Wraith scene at round ~8 (game still live), 96/124 are verified backups; the stale hunt tail (13, 7, 99, 3, 17, 5, 23, 24) is dropped. Driver policy untouched; zero engine/tunable/data changes (scripts/shots-v3.mjs only). shots:v3 now captures 7/7 and exits 0 on repeat runs. STANDING NOTE: this seed is tunable- and topology-specific and has now been retired three times (T-222/T-224, T-231/T-236, T-239) — ANY future board or doom-pacing change must re-run `npm run shots:v3` and re-sweep the Wraith seed as part of that change, and record it here.

MEASURED 2026-07-20 (T-227) — POST-TOPOLOGY BALANCE, recorded-not-tuned. The 21-node true 8-spoke board voids the old §9 lock; the fresh 2-seed baseline (sim-results/v3-21node-baseline-n40/) REPLACES it. Competitive pooled dark win 53.4%/52.3% (+34pp vs the 18.9/18.3% voided lock), doom/attrition 53.1/0.3 & 52.0/0.2 (doom +34.9pp; attrition FELL to 0.5% share), rounds 10.62/10.68, captures 1.32/1.42 with 33.1/35.6% ransom-back (capture economy HEALTHY); BP triple traitor-win/exposure/accuracy 33.1/62.8/79.3 & 36.1/59.7/81.1 (traitor win ~doubled). VERDICT: the miss is STRUCTURAL doom-pacing, NOT the two named 4-spoke failure modes (attrition-dominant wins = NO, attrition share dropped; dead capture economy = NO, alive) — the +4 mid-belt nodes + 8 blight seams give the dark a denser/faster doom front (doom completes 3x more often, sooner). A re-lock to 18-22% is NOT a clean single-knob tunable; it needs a topology-aware doom recalibration, DEFERRED to post-playtest/V4 (out of M2.5 scope). Zero tunable-VALUE edits this milestone.
